data.title: Hello, world from Sinatra
data.excerpt: A blog post stored as a JSON file in @webhouse/cms and rendered by a Sinatra route.
data.content:
## The setup

This post lives at `content/posts/hello-world.json`. It's a flat JSON file in the @webhouse/cms format. **Sinatra reads it directly** via the `Webhouse::Reader` class — no API calls, no database, just `Pathname#glob` and `JSON.parse`.

## Rails compatible

The `webhouse.rb` file in this example is a drop-in module. To use it in a Rails app, copy it to `app/lib/webhouse.rb`, then call `Webhouse::Reader.new(Rails.root.join('content'))` from your controller. The API is identical.

## Why this matters

The content is in your git repository. The schema is in `cms.config.ts`. The renderer is your Ruby app. There's no vendor lock-in.
data.author: Christian Broberg
